channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: How Audiomack became an unlikely Spotify competitor
item.description: Kendrick Lamar. Drake. Lady Gaga. The charts of music streaming services pretty much all look the same these days, with familiar names dominating the top spots—except on up-and-coming Spotify competitor Audiomack. The current No.
item.dc:creator: Janko Roettgers, Fast Company

item.title: Alibaba launches rival to DeepSeek AI a day after Manus unveiling
item.description: The Chinese tech giant touted its new model, QwQ-32B, as delivering “exceptional performance, almost entirely surpassing OpenAI-o1-mini.
item.dc:creator: CNN.com Wire Service, The Mercury News

item.title: Analysis: Wall Street is turning its back on Elon Musk
item.description: Liberals and government employees aren’t the only people angry with Elon Musk. Tesla’s plunging share price means many investors are angry, too.
item.dc:creator: CNN.com, The Mercury News

item.title: New survey suggests the vast majority of iPhone and Samsung Galaxy users find AI useless – and to be honest, I’m not surprised
item.description: A survey of iPhone and Samsung Galaxy users has revealed that most don’t care about AI whatsoever, despite both companies putting AI at the core of their mobile software and marketing throughout the past year.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: Upcoming DJI Mavic 4 Pro premium drone could deliver new camera skills and LiDAR – here’s what the latest leaks tell us
item.description: A leaked render apparently showing the clear final design of the DJI Mavic 4 Pro suggests redesigned propellers are coming.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Scam alert: This is how thieves might try to steal your tax refund or other money
item.description: The IRS just released its "dirty dozen," an annual list of the top 12 ways tax filers could get ripped off. It also lists common mistakes to avoid making when filing taxes.
item.dc:creator: Roxana Popescu, The Mercury News

item.title: Salesforce Introduces Agentblazer Status to Trailhead with AI Learning Roadmap
item.description: Salesforce has introduced Agentblazer Status to its Trailhead online learning platform, recognizing three status levels in the development of Agentforce skills and culminating in the new Agentforce Specialist certification.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Prep 107GB of space as Assassin's Creed Shadows preload and expected global release times are shared by Ubisoft
item.description: Ubisoft has announced the global release times for Assassin's Creed Shadows, and you can preload it right now.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Ecco come i leader IT si preparano alla grande sfida per la gestione degli agenti AI
item.description: Via via che gli AI agent arriveranno a occupare porzioni sempre più significative dei flussi di lavoro aziendali, i leader IT dovranno affrontare una sfida sempre più complessa: gestirli.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Utah becomes the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify ages
item.description: Utah has become the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify users’ ages and get parental consent for minors to download apps to their devices.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Schoenbaum, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Apple appeals UK encryption backdoor demand
item.description: Apple has publicly been pushing back against a UK order that demands it create encryption backdoors, and it has now formally appealed that order to the UK Investigatory Powers Tribunal.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Why “café-like branches” are the latest buzz among CIOs
item.description: In the fast-paced world of technology, it’s rare for an idea to capture the attention of CIOs and IT leaders universally—but the concept of the “café-like branch” has done just that.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: C++ founder champions profiles for memory safety
item.description: C++ memory safety issues have come under heat recently, with rising interest in projects like TrapC and Safe C++ that attempt to tackle the issue. But C++ designer Bjarne Stroustrup remains steadfast in promoting profiles as essential for safety in C++.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft launches genAI sales agents, also focuses on finance and supply chain
item.description: Microsoft is planning a string of new AI agents to automate mundane tasks and improve worker productivity. The company on Wednesday announced two AI-based sales agents that can autonomously follow up on sales leads.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Pentagon signs AI deal to aid military decision-making
item.description: The Department of Defense has signed a contract with start-up Scale AI to use artificial intelligence for military planning and operations, marking the Pentagon's latest incorporation of emerging tech into its workflows.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Apple boosts Mac Studio, MacBook Air with super-powered processors
item.description: Apple’s spring season product launches continues. The company today followed the introduction of its new M4 iPad Air with two additional releases, a MacBook Air with an M4 chip and the latest super-powered upgrades to its most powerful Mac, the Mac Studio.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Judge denies Elon Musk's request to block OpenAI for-profit conversion but welcomes trial
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (AP) — A federal judge has denied Elon Musk's request for a court order blocking OpenAI from converting itself to a for-profit company but said she could expedite a trial to consider Musk's claims against the ChatGPT maker and its CEO. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: UK watchdog drops competition review of Microsoft's OpenAI deal
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Britain's competition watchdog said Wednesday it has dropped its review of Microsoft's partnership with OpenAI, saying it's satisfied that the deal doesn't need a closer investigation under the country's merger rules.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: AI pioneers who channeled 'hedonistic' machines win computer science's top prize
item.description: Teaching machines in the way that animal trainers mold the behavior of dogs or horses has been an important method for developing artificial intelligence and one that was recognized Wednesday with the top computer science award.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Reddit co-founder Alexis Ohanian joins bid to acquire TikTok
item.description: Reddit co-founder Alexis Ohanian has joined billionaire Frank McCourt’s bid to acquire TikTok as a strategic adviser.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Georgia lawmakers push to ban automated speed cameras near schools
item.description: DECATUR, Ga. (AP) — Outside Beacon Hill Middle School in the Atlanta suburb of Decatur, like along hundreds of roadsides across Georgia, the unblinking eye of a camera tickets drivers who speed through a school zone.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: SolarWinds buys Squadcast to speed incident response
item.description: Observability provider SolarWinds announced this week it had signed an agreement to acquire San Francisco-based Squadcast and its incident response technology for an undisclosed amount.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Minnesota considers blocking 'nudify' apps that use AI to make explicit images without consent
item.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. (AP) — Molly Kelley was stunned to discover in June that someone she knew had used widely available “nudification” technology to create highly realistic and sexually explicit videos and images of her, using family photos that were posted on social media.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Musk to meet with House Republicans to talk DOGE on Wednesday
item.description: Elon Musk is scheduled to meet with House Republicans in the Capitol on Wednesday evening, multiple sources confirmed to The Hill, to provide an update on the work of the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE). The huddle is scheduled to take place at the Capitol at 7 p.m. EST.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, The Hill

item.title: Employee screening data breach exposes 3.3 million records
item.description: Major employee screening company data breach affects 3.3 million people. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson explains what happened and gives five ways to protect your data.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: German mobile network goes all-in on AI
item.description: With the AI Phone and Magenta AI, Deutsche Telekom is helping to shape further developments in the smartphone sector.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Jamf Acquires Identity Automation, Enhancing Platform Security on Apple Devices
item.description: Jamf acquires Identity Automation for $215 million, expanding its platform security capabilities and enabling dynamic identity and access management across various industries.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Why genAI-powered intelligent document processing is a big deal
item.description: One of the earliest digital transformation drivers was digitalization, in which organizations converted paper-based processes to digitized workflows. In ideal circumstances, paper documents became web or mobile forms, and workflows replaced handoffs.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: TypeScript 5.8 reaches general availability
item.description: Microsoft’s TypeScript 5.8, the latest version of the company’s strongly typed superset of JavaScript, has reached general availability (GA).
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Your iPhone has a hidden folder eating up storage space without you even knowing
item.description: iPhones have a hidden folder that eats up storage without you knowing it. Kurt the CyberGuy offers some tricks to free up storage on your device.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Microsoft shutting down Skype in May
item.description: Microsoft is closing down Skype, the video-calling service it bought for $8.5 billion in 2011, which had helped spark a transformation in how people communicate online.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: AI & Data Exchange 2025: Lawrence Livermore’s Brian Spears, Judy Hill on achieving unprecedented AI advancements
item.description: Powerful computing platforms let Lawrence Livermore evolve artificial intelligence to ultimately benefit government and Americans, lab’s top AI researchers say.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Obis, Federal News Network

item.title: Network jobs watch: Hiring, skills and certification trends
item.description: Network and infrastructure roles continue to shift as enterprises adopt technologies such as AI-driven network operations, multicloud networking, zero trust network access (ZTNA), and SD-WAN.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: AI & Data Exchange 2025: IBM’s Ryan Macaleer on AI tools becoming more fluid, adaptable
item.description: Artificial intelligence capabilities are becoming more robust and can expedite agency decision-making, says the IBM Federal software leader. The post AI & Data Exchange 2025: IBM’s Ryan Macaleer on AI tools becoming more fluid, adaptable first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network
